skill.name: c4-architecture
skill.description: Create C4 model architecture documentation with Mermaid diagrams for system context, containers, components, deployment views, and dynamic flows.
skill.summary: Create C4 Mermaid architecture diagrams for codebases and software systems.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell, network, sensitive data, cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found Markdown and Mermaid C4 diagram syntax rather than executable code. No prompt injection, credential access, shell execution, or outbound network behavior was found. The remaining risk is expected workspace file creation under docs/architecture/.

low_findings.title: Expected Documentation File Writes
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to write generated architecture documentation to docs/architecture/. This is expected behavior for a documentation skill, but it can modify workspace files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly define output filenames under docs/architecture/. The behavior is explicit, bounded to documentation output, and not hidden.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Matches Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Backticks are Markdown and Mermaid fences, sensitive terms are architecture labels, IP ranges are sample VPC CIDRs, and URLs point to C4 and Mermaid documentation. No executable shell or network request behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed the flagged patterns occur inside documentation examples and reference links. I found no code path that executes commands, reads secrets, or performs network calls.

content.value_statement: Architecture is hard to explain when diagrams mix audiences, scopes, and deployment details. This skill creates C4 Mermaid documentation tailored to context, containers, components, deployments, and flows.
actual_capabilities: Creates C4 Context, Container, Component, Deployment, and Dynamic diagram guidance in Mermaid syntax.
Maps audiences to appropriate diagram levels for executives, product managers, architects, developers, and DevOps teams.
Explains Mermaid C4 element, boundary, relationship, styling, and layout syntax.
Documents common C4 modeling mistakes with corrected examples.
Provides patterns for microservices, event-driven systems, API gateways, OAuth flows, and cloud deployments.
limitations: Does not execute diagram rendering or validate Mermaid output automatically.
Generated architecture depends on codebase access and user-provided context.
Does not replace human architecture review for security, compliance, or production readiness.
Mermaid C4 support omits some PlantUML C4 features.

use_cases.title: Explain a System to Stakeholders
use_cases.description: Create context and container diagrams that show users, external systems, applications, databases, and key relationships.

use_cases.title: Document Service Boundaries
use_cases.description: Model microservices, ownership boundaries, queues, databases, and external integrations for technical planning.

use_cases.title: Describe Production Deployment
use_cases.description: Create deployment diagrams that show cloud nodes, load balancers, application instances, and managed databases.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Context Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a high-level system view.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a C4 System Context diagram for this application. Show primary users, the system boundary, external systems, and important relationships.

prompt_templates.title: Create Container Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for technical architecture documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this repository and create C4 Context and Container diagrams. Write concise explanations for each container and relationship.

prompt_templates.title: Model Microservices and Events
prompt_templates.prompt: Create C4 diagrams for our microservices platform. Show team-owned systems, service containers, databases, event topics, and cross-service relationships.

prompt_templates.title: Produce Full Architecture Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Create architecture documentation under docs/architecture/. Include context, container, useful component diagrams, deployment view, and dynamic flows for critical requests.

output_examples.input: Create architecture diagrams for a workout tracker app.
output: A System Context diagram showing the user, the workout tracker application, and browser storage.
A Container diagram showing the single-page app, state management, and local IndexedDB storage.
Short notes explaining why deeper component diagrams are optional for this small system.
A Context diagram showing customer, admin, order, product, inventory, payment, and fulfillment systems.
Container diagrams for owned services with databases, queues, APIs, and external dependencies.
Guidance to model individual event topics instead of one generic message bus.

output_examples.input: Show how users sign in through our web app.
output: A Dynamic diagram with numbered authentication steps between the web app, API, identity provider, and user database.
Relationship labels that describe token exchange, validation, and user data access.
A focused diagram that avoids unrelated infrastructure details.

best_practices: Start with Context and Container diagrams before adding component or deployment detail.
Use clear relationship labels that describe actions, data, and protocols.
Keep each diagram focused and split complex systems into smaller views.
anti_patterns: Do not model classes, packages, or shared libraries as deployable containers.
Do not show one generic message bus when individual topics or queues carry different flows.
Do not create every C4 level when the audience only needs a higher-level view.

faq.question: What diagram formats does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses Mermaid C4 syntax for Context, Container, Component, Deployment, and Dynamic diagrams.

faq.question: Can it analyze an existing repository?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect available project structure and source files to infer systems, containers, components, and relationships.

faq.question: Where does it write documentation?
faq.answer: The skill recommends writing architecture files under docs/architecture/ with names for context, containers, components, deployment, and flows.

faq.question: Does it render diagrams automatically?
faq.answer: No. It generates Mermaid diagram text and Markdown documentation, but rendering depends on your Mermaid-compatible tooling.

faq.question: Is it suitable for executives?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends System Context diagrams for executives and adds technical detail only for technical audiences.

faq.question: Does it support deployment diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes C4 Deployment guidance for cloud nodes, subnets, load balancers, services, and databases.
